cross_runtime_findings.summary: Bare names can collide across modules in effect queries (A.f vs B.f).
cross_runtime_findings.evidence: UNIQUE(module_id,name) permits duplicates; effects_db resolves by name LIMIT 1
cross_runtime_findings.fix: ACCEPTED (documented): name-based identity is the system-wide convention (callee_name is already unqualified; specs/arch-mcp-server.md documents the limitation). The previous qualified form broke ALL transitive joins and corrupted pure-fns; unqualified trades that for rare duplicate-name collisions. Follow-up logged: id-keyed effects (function_id + file_path joins).

cross_runtime_findings.summary: Sidecar contract (fn: "Module.function_name") would no longer match extractor rows, creating spurious NoEffect rows.
cross_runtime_findings.evidence: capability_db fn_exists exact-match; docs/attack-surface-capability.md:81 documents qualified form
cross_runtime_findings.fix: APPLIED: fn_exists accepts exact OR unqualified-suffix match; doc updated to state both forms match.

notes: Root cause: producer name-qualification mismatch (investigation brief). Fix aligns arch_effects_ocaml with arch_index_cmt naming; selftest-effects added to CI as ratchet; stale fallback exe path fixed. HIGH finding accepted-with-documentation (pre-existing system convention), MEDIUM fixed with backward-compatible suffix matching.
